symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe lumbar/pelvic muscle spasm on standing after prolonged sitting
symptoms.character: deep locked spasm, protective muscle splinting

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute flare of axial spondyloarthritis with muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical precipitant on a background of enthesitis produces painful paraspinal splinting.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden mechanical onset is compatible with disc herniation but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep bilateral SI/lumbar ache with NO radicular radiation below the knee

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral sacroiliac-region pain is typical of axial spondyloarthritis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of dermatomal below-knee radiation argues against radiculopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bowel/bladder function intact (no incontinence or retention)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact sphincter function makes cauda equina much less likely, though it does not exclude an evolving lesion.
Absent saddle anaesthesia on history reduces cauda equina probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new lower-limb weakness on history (movement limited by pain, not power)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No motor weakness on history argues against neurological compression, pending examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inflammatory back pattern — morning stiffness improving with movement; HLA-B27 positive; normal plain radiographs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammatory back pain (worse with rest, better with movement) plus HLA-B27 and normal radiographs defines nr-axSpA.

symptoms.symptom_name: No significant trauma and no osteoporosis history

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of trauma and fragility-fracture risk makes a compression fracture unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal lower-limb power (5/5) and reflexes on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured normal motor/reflex examination — SEALED; cannot be obtained in telehealth, which is precisely why same-day in-person review is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative straight-leg-raise (provokes back pain, not shooting leg pain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute lumbar disc herniation with radiculopathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-performed provocation test excluding radiculopathy — SEALED; not obtainable remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked bilateral paraspinal muscle spasm and exquisite sacroiliac joint tenderness on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician palpation finding supporting sacroiliac/paraspinal source — SEALED; not obtainable remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs: BP 142/88, HR 100, RR 18, SpO2 100%, afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute flare of axial spondyloarthritis with muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and blood-pressure elevation are non-specific, attributable to severe pain and anxiety; afebrile argues against spinal infection. Clinician-measured — SEALED.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of intact sphincter function, intact saddle sensation and no leg weakness makes cauda equina unlikely on history — but does NOT substitute for the neurological examination telehealth cannot perform.
Bilateral SI-region pain with morning stiffness improving on movement, HLA-B27 positive and normal radiographs is the nr-axSpA pattern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Focused history red-flag screen (bowel/bladder, saddle sensation, leg weakness, radicular radiation, trauma)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only 'investigation' the AI Doctor can perform remotely; must be completed before any management to exclude cauda equina on history.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day in-person neurological examination and straight-leg-raise
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to complete exclusion of cauda equina, radiculopathy and fracture, since these cannot be examined via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: MRI of the sacroiliac joints / lumbar spine (outpatient, non-urgent)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms active sacroiliitis in nr-axSpA and informs rheumatology management; NOT required acutely and should not be ordered urgently in the absence of red flags.
Supports monitoring of axial spondyloarthritis disease activity; a background/outpatient investigation, not an acute one.
Not indicated with a negative red-flag screen; ordering it reflexively is over-investigation. It becomes indicated only if a cauda equina red flag appears.
